spanish: Antes de que termine de decir este enunciado, de seguro ya adivinaste de dónde soy.
english: Before I finish saying this sentence, you surely already guessed where I'm from.
explanation: Present subjunctive (termine) after time conjunction 'antes de que' referring to future action

spanish: A mí me pasa cada rato cuando me junto con mi amiga argentina.
english: It happens to me all the time when I get together with my Argentine friend.
explanation: Indicative used after 'cuando' for habitual actions in present tense

spanish: Es una forma de decir: 'Ey, soy como tú'.
english: It's a way of saying: 'Hey, I'm like you.'
explanation: Impersonal 'es' + infinitive construction used to describe general ways of doing things
